title: How long would it take Funimation to Release 333
display_title: How long would it take Funimation to Release 333
wikitext:
[]: [SPOILERS] How long would it take Funimation to Release 3.33             Quote Post by TheDrmcvey » Thu Feb 21, 2013 10:24 pm Do any of you know by how many months?

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Fri Feb 22, 2013 2:14 am To do the recording & actually make the disc? Not long at all. The actors are cheap & do dubbing as a job. They probably can knock out their usual 'impressive' work on 3.33 in less than two weeks. Then Funimation will probably want to have another super limited theatrical run again & then put out the disc. But what will ultimately hold up the stateside release of 3.33 is up to the Khara legal team. When they decide it's okay for nations outside Japan to start distributing their film is when we'll get some real development on 3.33 in the US. We gotta wait till Eva 3.0 has run it's financial course in Japan & then some movements will be made on getting it to us. Thankfully though it seems Khara is in more of a mindset to get 3.0 out & move on to Final. Not that it matters BUT if Khara allows Funimation to distribute 3.0 within the year & if they give it a theatrical run it would mean 3.0 would meet all the criteria to be nominated for an Oscar.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri Feb 22, 2013 2:36 am The fast release of Q on DVD/BD in Japan follows the simultaneous release of the English version alongside the Japanese of the latest volume of Sadamoto's manga. Together these could be signalling a less uptight attitude to foreign release.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Fri Feb 22, 2013 8:26 pm Gendo'sPapa wrote: Not that it matters BUT if Khara allows Funimation to distribute 3.0 within the year & if they give it a theatrical run it would mean 3.0 would meet all the criteria to be nominated for an Oscar.

[]:             Quote Post by Nando » Fri Feb 22, 2013 9:09 pm Anywhere from December 2013 to February 2014 is my estimate. Funimation really came through with a surprisingly fast (two months after Japanese DVD/BD) release for Fullmetal Alchemist: The Sacred Star of Milos , but I am really not counting on that happening again. The reverse-importation risk is a lot higher for this case, I'd think. Actually dubbing it won't be any problem. Spike Spencer, Tiffany Grant, Trina Nishimura, Jerry Jewell and Allison Keith could probably knock out the main roles in a week or less, and everyone else is so minor that their roles probably would just take the weekend, even the new characters who have to be cast for the first time.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Feb 22, 2013 10:57 pm Nando wrote: Actually dubbing it won't be any problem. Spike Spencer, Tiffany Grant, Trina Nishimura, Jerry Jewell and Allison Keith could probably knock out the main roles in a week or less, and everyone else is so minor that their roles probably would just take the weekend, even the new characters who have to be cast for the first time.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Feb 22, 2013 11:31 pm Gendo'sPapa wrote: But what will ultimately hold up the stateside release of 3.33 is up to the Khara legal team.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sat Feb 23, 2013 11:47 am NEVER. ANNO'S GOING TO NEVER RELEASE IT TO THE USA AND WE'RE ALL GOING TO DIE AND TURN INTO TANG AND SEE NAKED REIS EVERYWHERE.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Feb 23, 2013 3:07 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: NEVER. ANNO'S GOING TO NEVER RELEASE IT TO THE USA AND WE'RE ALL GOING TO DIE AND TURN INTO TANG AND SEE NAKED REIS EVERYWHERE.

[]:             Quote Post by Nando » Mon Feb 25, 2013 4:03 am Kendrix wrote: I wonder how they'll take it, seeing that the original cast was partially thrown into despair. The work on Rebuild has been pretty good (especially the returning actors... even Grant sucked a little less - You lucky ppl, I feel it was the other way around with the German dubs, with the original series sounding much better, even if they just couldn't get the names pronounced right. But there's still the returning actors. At least Shinji has one of the returning actors... ), but the original performances leave quite some shoes to fill, and let's face it, some characters, especially Shinji(because of the controversity potential of his actions) and Misato (The right balance between cruel and conflicted)need to be absolutely nailed lest they look like total jerks or the scenes get narmy where they absolutely shouldn't. It's not that the English cast (The Rebuild cast, anyway) is that bad, it's that Q is hard to do/high demand. In a way, the exceptional original performance was required .

[]:             Quote Post by Güf Trüp » Fri Mar 01, 2013 6:34 pm My main concern is whether or not Jerry Jewell can pull off Kaworu, since he's spent the last two movies giving him a "Creepy Stalker"-ish vibe, which just won't work this time around.

[]:             Quote Post by SurferJon » Fri Mar 01, 2013 9:43 pm I asked Spike Spencer at this past AX how long it took him to record for 2.0 and he told me just one day. He flew out to Texas and knocked it our pretty quickly. I don't think it takes them that long to record each actor.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri Mar 01, 2013 10:01 pm SurferJon wrote: I asked Spike Spencer at this past AX how long it took him to record for 2.0 and he told me just one day. He flew out to Texas and knocked it our pretty quickly. I don't think it takes them that long to record each actor.

[]:             Quote Post by The Killer of Heroes » Sat Mar 02, 2013 12:19 am Kendrix wrote: I feel bad for Kaji's Rebuild actor, he was really getting to the role, and his perception of Kaji/description at some interview made me look at him in a new way.
